sql: SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department
SELECT COUNT(*) AS workforce FROM employees
SELECT region, AVG(performance_score) AS avg_performance FROM employees WHERE active = TRUE GROUP BY region
SELECT job_family, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees WHERE region IN ('France', 'Germany') GROUP BY job_family
SELECT name FROM employees WHERE name = 'Alice'
SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department
SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM employees WHERE region = 'France' OR region = 'Spain' GROUP BY department
SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(*) = 1
SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM (SELECT * FROM employees) GROUP BY department
SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY department UNION SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM employees GROUP BY department
SELECT department, SUM(salary) AS payroll FROM employees GROUP BY department
